name: Moodboard — Staggered Grid
description: Grid of images/colors with staggered fade-in, soft shadows, and slight rotation randomness. Creates an editorial moodboard feel — each cell fades in sequentially with a subtle rotation offset, building visual texture.

rotation_range.description: Max rotation in degrees (applied deterministically per cell)

items.accent_color: string (optional, CSS color — used as background while loading)

sub_primitives.name: Moodboard Stagger Fade
sub_primitives.description: Sequential fade-in with slight rotation. Each cell enters with opacity 0→1 and a small rotation settle.
sub_primitives.css: @keyframes moodboard-cell-in { from { opacity: 0; transform: rotate(var(--rot)) scale(0.95); } to { opacity: 1; transform: rotate(var(--rot)) scale(1); } }

when_to_use: Visual inspiration boards — showing design references or style tiles
when_to_avoid: Small content sets (fewer than 4 items) — grid feels sparse
Rapid-fire sequences where individual images don't need attention
ai_guidance: Use layer type 'moodboard' with an 'items' array of { src, alt?, accent_color? }. Configure grid via moodboard_config overrides. The stagger runs ~3s through all cells.

name: Result Grid — Row Fill
description: Search results or cards in a responsive grid that fills row by row. Each card slides in with a stagger, and an optional highlight pulse draws attention to a selected card.

card_height.description: Height of each card in pixels

highlight_index.description: Index of card to highlight (-1 for none)

sub_primitives.name: Result Row Fill
sub_primitives.description: Cards enter row by row with translateY + opacity stagger.
sub_primitives.css: @keyframes result-card-in { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(16px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); } }

sub_primitives.name: Result Highlight Pulse
sub_primitives.description: Subtle border glow pulse on the highlighted card.
sub_primitives.css: @keyframes result-highlight { 0% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 rgba(99,102,241,0); } 50% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(99,102,241,0.4); } 100% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 rgba(99,102,241,0); } }

when_to_use: Search result displays in product demos
Dashboard card layouts filling in with data
when_to_avoid: Single-item focus — too much grid infrastructure for one card
Very long lists (>12 items) — grid becomes crowded
ai_guidance: Use layer type 'result_grid' with an 'items' array of { title, subtitle?, thumbnail?, badge? }. Configure via grid_config overrides. Set highlight_index to draw attention to a specific result.

name: Stacked Thumbs — Fan Reveal
description: Overlapping thumbnails stacked at slight offsets, fanning out to reveal all items. Creates a casual, hand-dealt card feel — items start piled up and spread apart with rotation offsets.

stack_offset_x.description: Horizontal offset per stacked item in pixels

stack_offset_y.description: Vertical offset per stacked item in pixels

fan_angle.description: Max rotation angle when fanned out (degrees)

sub_primitives.name: Stack Fan Out
sub_primitives.description: Cards spread from stacked position to fanned layout with spring physics.
sub_primitives.css: @keyframes stack-fan { from { transform: translate(0, 0) rotate(0deg); } to { transform: translate(var(--fan-x), 0) rotate(var(--fan-rot)); } }

when_to_use: Photo stack reveals — showing a collection of images
Media library thumbnails fanning out for selection
when_to_avoid: More than 6-8 items — overlap becomes unreadable
Text-heavy content — thumbnails work best with images
Grids or structured layouts — use result_grid or moodboard instead
ai_guidance: Use layer type 'stacked_thumbs' with an 'items' array of { src, label? }. Configure via thumbs_config overrides. The fan animation runs ~2s with spring physics.

name: Media Strip — Horizontal Scroll
description: Horizontal scrolling filmstrip of images or video thumbnails. Continuous scroll with optional pause-at points for editorial pacing. Creates a cinematic film reel or timeline scrubbing feel.

item_width.description: Width of each strip item in pixels

scroll_speed.description: Scroll speed in pixels per second

pause_at.description: Array of indices to pause at briefly during scroll

pause_duration_ms.description: Duration of each pause in milliseconds

item_height.description: Height of each strip item in pixels

strip_y.description: Vertical position of the strip center

sub_primitives.name: Strip Scroll
sub_primitives.description: Continuous horizontal scroll with eased pause points. Physics-driven for smooth deceleration at pause markers.

when_to_use: Film reel / timeline scrubbing through content
Horizontal gallery browsing in product demos
Sizzle reel B-roll showing multiple screenshots or frames
when_to_avoid: Fewer than 4 items — strip feels empty
Content requiring focused reading — scroll moves too quickly
Vertical layouts — this is inherently horizontal
ai_guidance: Use layer type 'media_strip' with an 'items' array of { src, caption? }. Configure via strip_config overrides. Set pause_at to indices where the scroll should briefly pause. The scroll runs ~4s.
